source_jobs.title: Operational Compliance Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CERTN

At Certn, we’re changing how trust works with The World’s Easiest Background Check. We’ve raised $127M+, earned Deloitte Fast 50 recognition three years in a row, and we’re still only scratching the surface.

Our goal is straightforward: help people move faster - into jobs, homes, and opportunities - by simplifying the path to trust. We’re not a traditional background screening company.

We’re a team of curious, collaborative builders who care about solving real problems for real people. We challenge each other, move fast, and have fun doing it.

If you want to grow, make an impact, and help shape products used by millions, this is your place. Let’s build what’s next, together.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting directly to Senior Counsel of Privacy, the Operational Compliance Specialist will play a key part in Certn’s privacy and EMEA compliance programs, by translating legal and regulatory requirements into reliable, repeatable operational processes. This role will focus on managing subprocessor compliance, data retention, client privacy communications, EMEA reporting obligations, and technical compliance. You will serve as a critical partner across the business, ensuring privacy-related processes are effectively implemented, maintained, and aligned with evolving regulatory requirements.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Technical Privacy Compliance

 - Maintain the subprocessor register; manage the end-to-end process for reviewing, onboarding, and notifying clients of subprocessor changes in line with contractual and regulatory obligations.

 - Administer data retention schedules - tracking retention periods by data category and jurisdiction, coordinating deletion and archiving activities, and maintaining supporting documentation.

 - Support access control compliance, including maintaining records of data access permissions and assisting with periodic access reviews.

 - Own the end-to-end Data Subject Access Request (DSAR) intake process ensuring accurate request tracking, seamless coordination across internal stakeholders, and timely fulfillment in compliance with regulatory requirements and response deadlines.

 - Support the privacy office in monitoring and testing activities and audits, notably by ensuring proper documentation and tracking of the controls and related findings.

 - Ensure that compliance documentation remains up-to-date and support the management and handling of any relevant registries of processing activities and risks assessments.
   
   

Client Privacy Communications

 - Act as a first point of contact for client and applicant compliance queries relating to privacy and EMEA regulatory matters, triaging straightforward requests independently and escalating complex or legally sensitive issues to Senior Counsel as appropriate.

 - Prepare and issue subprocessor update notices and other privacy-related client communications.

 - Maintain client-specific compliance records relating to data processing agreements and privacy obligations.
   
   

EMEA Regulatory Operations

 - Track EMEA jurisdiction-specific reporting requirements and filing deadlines; coordinate preparation in support of the Senior Counsel, Privacy and Senior Compliance Officer.

 - Maintain the EMEA compliance calendar and assist with regulatory correspondence.

 - Support credentialing and onboarding/offboarding compliance processes for EMEA clients and vendors.

 - Apply a working knowledge of regulated criminal-record screening across the UK disclosure regimes (DBS, Disclosure Scotland, and AccessNI), including the distinct check levels available under each and the eligibility rules that govern which level a given role qualifies for.

 - Understand Certn's obligations when acting as, or supporting clients who rely on, a registered body, umbrella body, or responsible organisation, including the duty to confirm role eligibility, verify applicant identity, and handle disclosure results in line with each agency's code of practice.

 -

source_jobs.title: Intermediate Developer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CERTN

At Certn, we’re changing how trust works with The World’s Easiest Background Check. We’ve raised $127M+, earned Deloitte Fast 50 recognition three years in a row, and we’re still only scratching the surface. Our goal is straightforward: help people move faster - into jobs, homes, and opportunities - by simplifying the path to trust.

 

We’re not a traditional background screening company. We’re a team of curious, collaborative builders who care about solving real problems for real people. We challenge each other, move fast, and have fun doing it. If you want to grow, make an impact, and help shape products used by millions, this is your place. Let’s build what’s next, together.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Intermediate Developer, you'll be involved in all software development activities as part of an Agile team, including estimating, sprint planning, software development life cycle activities, code reviews, research, deployments, and documentation. You'll also provide technical leadership and mentorship to junior team members, helping ensure the delivery of high-quality software that aligns with customer needs, technical architecture, development standards, and quality practices.





KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Software Development (70%)

 - Contribute as a developer in an agile team, collaborating with technical and non-technical team members, and working closely with database, back-end, and front-end stacks

 - Ensure software deliverables align with the technical architecture, development standards, and quality practices

 - Assist in building and executing integrated unit tests for all code modules

 - Develop well-written, intent-revealing code

 - Assist in ensuring observability of the team's code and products, and monitor them in a live environment, ensuring high availability and reliability

 - Produce thorough documentation outlining high-level architecture, flow, sequence diagrams, blockers, risks, and other technical considerations as needed

 - Work on bugs that need immediate attention to unblock our customers


Teamwork & Collaboration (30%)

 - Participate in peer review activities

 - Participate in a learning environment through collaboration, shared knowledge, and leadership

 - Work within the agile team to gather production metrics and stakeholder feedback and use them to iterate and grow team projects

 - Mentor and guide junior developers

 - Collaborate with senior developers to work on features of a big project

 - Collaborate with product managers to get clarity on project requirements




KEY QUALIFICATIONS

 - Strong understanding of relational databases and their usage in software development

 - Strong proficiency in Python

 - Demonstrated working experience in software development, with a focus on delivering high-quality software

 - Have delivered at least one feature end-to-end in collaboration with other developers

 - Basic understanding of REST APIs and their function in web development

 - Excellent written and verbal communication, with the ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams and stakeholders




BONUS

 - Proven expertise in Django and Django Rest Framework

 - Experience delivering SaaS software in an agile environment

 - Experience collaborating with product managers to define and clarify requirements, and ensuring successful delivery of software products

 - Experience building and maintaining RESTful APIs, with an understanding of best practices for authentication and authorization mechanisms

 - Experience deploying, configuring, and troubleshooting deployed code on Amazon Web Services (AWS) in a professional/enterprise environment

 - Experience working with front-end frameworks such as React or Angular



If you meet most, but not all, of the qualifications listed above, we still encourage you to apply. We recognize that strong candidates come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences, and we value the diversity of perspectives that brings

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CERTN

At Certn, we’re changing how trust works with The World’s Easiest Background Check. We’ve raised $127M+, earned Deloitte Fast 50 recognition three years in a row, and we’re still only scratching the surface.

Our goal is straightforward: help people move faster - into jobs, homes, and opportunities - by simplifying the path to trust. We’re not a traditional background screening company.

We’re a team of curious, collaborative builders who care about solving real problems for real people. We challenge each other, move fast, and have fun doing it.

If you want to grow, make an impact, and help shape products used by millions, this is your place. Let’s build what’s next, together.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting directly to Senior Manager, Customer Success, the Enterprise, Customer Success Manager plays a strategic role in driving long-term customer success across a portfolio of enterprise clients. As a trusted advisor, you'll partner with customers to maximize value, drive adoption, and identify opportunities for growth, while collaborating cross-functionally to deliver an exceptional customer experience. This role owns the commercial relationship after onboarding, balancing customer advocacy with revenue growth through renewals, expansions, and strategic account planning.

 


HOW YOU WILL HAVE IMPACT:

Strategic Customer Success Initiative

 - Develop and execute customer success strategies to enhance satisfaction, onboarding, retention, and revenue growth.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align customer success initiatives with overall business objectives.

Customer Advocacy and Industry Insights

 - Act as a key advocate for customers within the organisation, ensuring their needs are understood and addressed.

 - Stay informed about industry trends and leverage insights to provide strategic recommendations to customers.

Performance Metrics and Reporting

 - Monitor customer health and performance metrics to identify opportunities, risks, and areas for optimization.

 - Deliver quarterly business reviews and provide actionable insights to both customers and internal stakeholders.

 - Utilise data-driven insights to identify opportunities for continuous improvement and innovation.

Customer Retention and Growth

 - Give input into and implement strategies to improve customer retention rates.

 - Identify and address factors that may contribute to customer churn.

 - Own the full commercial lifecycle post-onboarding, including renewals, upsells, and cross-sells, and identify and track expansion opportunities within the existing customer base.

 - Collaborate with sales and marketing teams to develop and implement strategies for customer expansion.

Mentorship to Junior Team Members

 - Help foster a culture of continuous learning, collaboration, and excellence within the team

 


WHAT YOU WILL BRING:

 - Experience in a customer success or account management role, including demonstrated experience advocating for customers at an executive level within the organisation.

 - Track record of driving customer growth through successful cross-selling and upselling initiatives.

 - Demonstrated ability to collaborate effectively with various departments for overall business success.

 - Working experience with SaaS products and the technology industry

 - Proficiency in utilising data analytics to drive insights and improvements.

 - Proven ability to provide strategic recommendations based on industry insights.


If you meet most, but not all, of the qualifications listed above, we still encourage you to apply. We recognize that strong candidates come from a wide range of backgrounds and experiences, and we value the diversity of perspectives that brings.




OUR CULTURE

Certn is a remote-first company with a high-performance edge. We value hustle, hunger, and helping each other win - but we also have a strict no-jerk policy. Ambition here is about lifting people up, not stepping on toes.

 - We think like o

source_jobs.title: Senior Ruby On Rails Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Zipline

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees—from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



About the Role

We're looking for a Senior Ruby on Rails Engineer to join our team as the surface area of our product and infrastructure continues to scale with rapid customer growth. This is a great opportunity for someone excited about shaping robust systems—and contributing to AI-powered initiatives such as automatic content creation.



What You’ll Do

 - Defining and improving useful APIs for our customers

 - Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable features for our web-based applications

 - Maintain and enhance existing features, adapting them for an evolving market

 - Evangelize coding, testing and performance best practices with the rest of the team

 - Collaborate with our product, design and customer success teams on new features that customers will love
   
   

What We're Looking For

 - You have significant relevant production experience with large-scale Ruby on Rails applications

 - You have great written and verbal communication skills

 - You prefer taking projects from inception to completion and are outcome-oriented

 - You have a strong understanding of systems architecture, database design, and the Ruby language

 - You are proactive with communication and have no problem managing your time as a remote employee

 - You are proud of your craft, and enjoy and value clean code that scales to keep large teams productive

 - You have an interest in—or experience with—AI/ML technologies, especially in content generation or data structuring



Technologies You'll Work With

 - Ruby on Rails

 - Postgres, Redis, Elasticsearch

 - TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Webpack

 - AWS, Heroku, Ansible, Digital Ocean

 - GitHub, Slack, Google Drive, JIRA

 - Claude Code
   
   

Compensation

At Zipline, we're committed to paying our team fairly, transparently, and in a way that supports growth. This role is targeting an annual salary within Canada of $143,555 CAD to $175,455 CAD.



The broad salary ranges reflect the growth someone will experience in the role—from developing skills to reaching expertise within their career level. We've found that hiring team members with the expectation of being accomplished in the role sets them up for success, which for this position on the Canadian band is $143,555 CAD. Salaries below this point typically reflect someone with potential but still building the necessary skills, while salaries above are for individuals already excelling in the role, with a deep understanding of Zipline's impact on customers, product, and company.



At Zipline, you'll continue to have career development conversations with your manager to ensure your compensation grows as you advance.



Our salary ranges are determined by market, factoring in our revenue and employee size and paying competitively. In higher cost of living markets, we apply 5-10% multipliers on our ranges, depending on the location, to maintain competitive compensation and reflective of the increased expenses our team members face in those areas. While we primarily hire in Canada and the USA, we are a global employer, and all hiring regions will use the same methodology to determine fair and transparent pay.



Transparency is key to building trust in our compensation process. To better understand how this range is determined, we encourage you to ask as many questions as you'd like about compensation during the interview process to ensure clarity and confidence.



What's In

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Specialist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON ONE

At Neon One, we believe that technology is the key to building vibrant communities of generosity. As a leader in nonprofit software since 2004, we create intuitive solutions that help small and mid-sized nonprofits connect with people, build trust, and make good happen every day.

Our culture is powered by empathy, innovation, and a shared mission to empower organizations making a difference. We operate with a customer-first mindset, take pride in extraordinary results, and grow together by supporting each other and embracing bold new ideas. If you’re passionate about using your skills to drive real impact and want to thrive in a collaborative, fully remote environment, Neon One is the place for you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Neon One is seeking a Customer Support Specialist for our Arts People platform. Arts People is a specialized, all-in-one ticketing, box office, and fundraising solution dedicated to empowering performing arts organizations. In this role, you will be the ultimate ally for creative nonprofits, helping them navigate everything from high-volume opening night ticket sales to complex patron database management. As part of our collaborative ecosystem, you will also be cross-trained to support other Neon One products.

We are seeking a self-motivated, puzzle-loving problem solver who thrives at the intersection of technology and the arts. As a Customer Support Specialist, you will deliver top-tier support through tickets, live chat, and phone support, ensuring our clients can focus on their initiatives, not troubleshooting software. This role is ideal for someone who loves deep technical troubleshooting, embraces a continuous learning mindset, and isn't afraid to ask questions to solve a complex issue. Above all, you must be passionate about delivering empathetic, best-in-class service to the nonprofit community.

At Neon One, we believe in the power of technology to amplify human impact. We are committed to intentionally leveraging AI to automate the routine, so our team can focus on what matters most: building personal connections and delivering extraordinary value to the nonprofit community. We don’t just use AI; we use it to be more human. We expect every team member—regardless of role—to embrace an 'innovator’s mindset,' exploring how emerging technologies can help us work smarter and serve our mission faster.



THE EXCITING PART

 - Serve as the first point of contact for arts organizations by answering questions and troubleshooting complex software workflows, including ticketing setup, individual show configuration, seasonal pricing structures, and annual patron rollovers

 - Provide high-quality support across multiple support channels, including tickets, live chat, and phone support

 - Proficiently navigate our support software (Zendesk) to document, answer, and escalate support tickets when needed

 - Deliver accurate, empathetic support while consistently meeting or exceeding key performance indicators (KPIs), including Time to First Response (TTFR), Customer Satisfaction (CSAT), and response and resolution time targets

 - Test and verify software bugs; follow procedures to communicate verified issues to support team leaders or product teams

 - Prioritize own workload effectively and with attention to detail

 - Research issues in Zendesk, Slackbot, and Jira to identify potential trends and prevent duplication of work within the department

 - Provide information to Development team about software defects with urgency; encourage clients to share new feature or product enhancement requests via our Product Feedback platform

 - Follow established security and risk management procedures




THE EXPECTED PART

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date support documentation as needed

 - Leverage modern AI tools, including Zendesk Copilot, Gemini, and integrated Slackbots, to streamline daily workflows, summarize complex tickets, and accelerate issue resolution

 - Participate in training sessions t

source_jobs.title: Social Media Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON ONE

At Neon One, we believe that technology is the key to building vibrant communities of generosity. As a leader in nonprofit software since 2004, we create intuitive solutions that help small and mid-sized nonprofits connect with people, build trust, and make good happen every day.

Our culture is powered by empathy, innovation, and a shared mission to empower organizations making a difference. We operate with a customer-first mindset, take pride in extraordinary results, and grow together by supporting each other and embracing bold new ideas. If you’re passionate about using your skills to drive real impact and want to thrive in a collaborative, fully remote environment, Neon One is the place for you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Reporting to the Director of Brand & Communications, you’ll be responsible for developing and executing organic social media strategies that grow brand awareness, engage our audience, and drive measurable business impact. You’ll lead content creation and produce high-performing graphic and video assets that support organic social campaigns.

As the go-to expert on social media management, you will manage content across all organic platforms, ensuring a consistent brand voice and messaging. This is a hands-on, fast-paced role for someone who can take an idea from concept to finished product—whether it’s a quick-turn short-form video or a multi-post educational series. All content you create must be deeply customer-centric, speaking directly to the needs, challenges, and goals of nonprofit professionals who are our end users.




RESPONSIBILITIES


SOCIAL MEDIA STRATEGY & EXECUTION

 - Full ownership of our social media strategy, from ideation to execution, reporting, and optimization.

 - Own content creation and curation for LinkedIn, Meta, and other relevant platforms focused on customer-centric content that addresses the real-world challenges nonprofit professionals face, focusing on their needs rather than our products.

 - Maintain a consistent brand voice while driving continued growth across following and engagement.

 - Develop a deep understanding of the nonprofit sector and our ICP to ensure all content feels authentic, resonates with our audience, and acts as an extension of Neon One's thought leadership.

 - Concept and execute social-first content, graphics, and lightweight video content (e.g., explainers, ads, how-tos, testimonials) to support storytelling, product marketing, and community engagement across platforms.

 - Manage daily organic social operations: scheduling, publishing, community engagement, and moderation.

 - Leverage AI tools (Gemini, Canva AI, CapCut AI features, etc.) to scale content production while maintaining high creative standards and brand consistency.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional teams to align social media initiatives with broader business goals.

 - Stay current on platform updates, emerging trends, and best practices to keep our social presence ahead of the curve.

 - Maintain exceptionally high standards for all copy and creative work—every post should be polished, error-free, and on-brand.
   
   


PERFORMANCE & ANALYTICS

 - Monitor and analyze performance metrics such as reach, engagement, CTR, follower growth, lead gen, and content resonance.

 - Translate insights into clear, actionable recommendations to optimize strategy and content in real-time.

 - Identify what's working, and what's not, and pivot quickly to stay aligned with evolving audience behavior and business goals.

 - Set benchmarks, create weekly reports, and communicate performance to internal stakeholders in a clear, compelling way.

 - Design and run A/B tests or experiments to refine post formats, messaging, and publishing cadence.




QUALIFICATIONS


MUST HAVES

 - Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, or a related field.

 - 2-4 years of experience owning B2B social media strategy, execution, and reporting.

 - Background in B2B marketing is required; SaaS

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer, Design Systems & Foundations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Zipline is the operating system for the frontline, used by hundreds of thousands of store associates and managers every day at some of the world's leading retail brands. As our product and design teams continue to grow, we're investing in the systems and foundations that help us build consistent, high-quality experiences at scale.

We're looking for a Staff Product Designer to lead our Design Systems & Foundations work. This is a senior individual contributor role focused on more than maintaining a component library. You'll build and evolve the design systems, product frameworks, and foundational infrastructure that support product development across Zipline.

Your work will help designers, engineers, and PMs move faster, make better decisions, and create more consistent experiences across the product. You'll partner closely with design leadership, engineering, and product teams to shape how Zipline builds and scales.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and evolve ZUI, Zipline's design system, including component architecture, design tokens, accessibility standards, documentation, and implementation quality. Partner closely with engineering to ensure the system evolves alongside our product and technical architecture.

 - Establish shared interaction patterns, mental models, and design principles that create consistency across the product. Translate learnings from product work into reusable systems, frameworks, and foundations that can scale across teams.

 - Build the documentation, guidance, and decision-making frameworks that help designers, engineers, and PMs work independently and consistently. Drive adoption of the design system and product foundations across the organization.

 - Contribute to complex product initiatives, influence long-term product direction, and help define how emerging technologies, including AI, are incorporated into our design practice and workflows.

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience leading or significantly contributing to a design system at scale

 - Strong systems thinking across interaction design, workflows, information architecture, and visual design

 - Proven ability to drive alignment and adoption across cross-functional teams

 - Excellent visual and interaction design craft

 - Strong partnership with engineering and understanding of implementation constraints

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and shaping long-term product foundations

 - Interest in exploring how AI can improve design workflows and product development

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience designing for frontline, retail, or operational workflows

 - Experience in a platform, systems, or other cross-cutting design role

 - Familiarity with modern design system governance and documentation practices

 - Experience creating structured design context that can be leveraged by AI tools

 


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role sits at the center of how Zipline designs and builds products. You'll help define the systems, standards, and foundations that influence work across the entire organization, creating leverage far beyond any single feature or product area.

 


COMPENSATION

At Zipline, we're committed to paying our team fairly, transparently, and in a way that supports growth. This role is targeting an annual salary within Canada of $157,616 CAD to $192,665 CAD.

The broad salary ranges reflect the growth someone will experience in the role—from developing s

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON ONE

At Neon One, we believe that technology is the key to building vibrant communities of generosity. As a leader in nonprofit software since 2004, we create intuitive solutions that help small and mid-sized nonprofits connect with people, build trust, and make good happen every day.

Our culture is powered by empathy, innovation, and a shared mission to empower organizations making a difference. We operate with a customer-first mindset, take pride in extraordinary results, and grow together by supporting each other and embracing bold new ideas. If you’re passionate about using your skills to drive real impact and want to thrive in a collaborative, fully remote environment, Neon One is the place for you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Executive at Neon One, you live and breathe Neon One’s Core Values and play a pivotal role in expanding our reach and enhancing our customer success by driving sales within the small to mid-sized nonprofit market. You are responsible for the full sales cycle, from converting qualified meetings into wins to contributing to customer retention. This role requires a proactive individual with a strong understanding of the nonprofit landscape, excellent relationship-building and negotiation skills, and a passion for enabling nonprofits to succeed.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Opportunity Conversion: Convert qualified meetings generated by the SDR team and strategic alliances into new customer wins, effectively positioning Neon One's solutions

 - Sales Cycle Management: Manage the entire sales cycle, from initial discovery and demonstration to negotiation and closing, ensuring a seamless experience for prospective customers

 - Pipeline Generation: Collaborate with SDRs and strategic partners to understand lead sources and contribute to strategies for generating new pipelines

 - Solution Positioning: Effectively articulate the value proposition of Neon One's platform and tailor solutions to address the specific needs and challenges of small to mid-sized nonprofits

 - Successful Onboarding & Handoff: Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure a seamless transition for new customers from sales to their dedicated account manager, contributing to increased customer satisfaction and retention

 - Market Awareness: Stay informed about industry trends, competitive landscape, and the evolving needs of small to mid-sized nonprofits to effectively position Neon One's solutions

 - Performance Tracking and Reporting: Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) related to sales activity, including conversion rates from qualified meetings, sales won, and pipeline contribution

 - Relationship Management: Cultivate strong relationships with prospective customers and collaborate effectively with internal teams and external partners




KEY QUALIFICATIONS

 - A Bachelor's Degree

 - Minimum 2+ years of experience in direct software sales (SaaS), account management, or a related role, with a proven track record of converting qualified leads into wins

 - Proven ability to manage a sales pipeline and achieve sales objectives

 - Excellent presentation, communication, and interpersonal skills

 - Strong negotiation and closing skills

 - Strong analytical skills and the ability to track and report on key performance metrics

 - Ability to work independently and collaboratively within a fast-paced environment

 - Proficiency in CRM software is a must

 - Willingness to travel occasionally for client meetings and events




WHY JOIN NEON ONE?

At Neon One, our values are how we show up every day. We make good happen by putting empathy and passion at the center of our work, using technology to uplift mission-driven organizations. We stand for our customers, act with care and intention in every decision, own the solution, and grow together. We innovate fearlessly, always exploring new ways to support our community and each other.





HOW WE HIRE

We use AI tools to support our recruitment process, i

source_jobs.title: Marketing Events Manager
source_jobs.description:
ROLE OVERVIEW

As the Marketing Events Manager, you will be the engine responsible for the end-to-end planning, flawless execution, and optimization of owned, internal, partner, and third-party sponsored events. This spans everything from large-scale trade shows with compelling booth activations to smaller, high-value 1:1 executive meeting programs.



You will work closely with cross-functional teams to create compelling activations, thought leadership, and awareness that promote our campaigns effectively. The ideal candidate is a metric-driven, disciplined professional who can bring creative vision while leveraging meticulous attention to detail and excellent project management to ensure every initiative delivers the best possible ROI and pipeline contribution.




WHAT YOU WILL DO

 - Create an annual events strategy and plan to drive awareness and pipeline with key target accounts and ICPs.

 - Drive the planning, execution, and optimization of third-party and partner-sponsored events, owned events, and internal events to drive engagement, generate leads, and enhance brand presence.

 - Maintain project deliverables and timelines in project management tools, demonstrating hyper-focused attention to detail to ensure flawless execution across numerous concurrent events.

 - Own all event logistics and production, including vendor negotiation and management, contract review, budget adherence, and on-site execution.

 - Work with sales and CS teams to identify key target accounts and contacts, select attendees for on-site meetings, and run pre-event know-before-you-go sessions.

 - Develop and execute multi-channel promotional campaigns (email, social media, website) to maximize attendance and engagement.

 - Partner with Content, Design, and Product Marketing to develop creative booth activations, customer stories, messaging, and event experiences that bring the brand story to life and ensure a high-quality attendee experience.

 - Partner with sales to ensure generated leads are routed correctly and worked by reps pre- and post-campaign.

 - Establish a centralized event reporting system using CRM and data tools (e.g., Salesforce) to track performance metrics, including MQA-to-SQA conversion, cost-per-lead (CPL), and pipeline influence, to prove event ROI.

 - Create post-event follow-up strategies to nurture leads and convert attendees into customers.

 - Support the broader Partner Marketing team as needed, leveraging event expertise to assist with regional or hosted event logistics.




ABOUT HIGHLIGHTTA

This confidential opportunity is presented by HighlightTA, your on-demand talent team. We make hiring easy with flexible, on-demand talent solutions. We partner with startups and scale-ups to embed talent partners, lead recruitment projects, and manage full talent functions. Our data-driven approach ensures fast hiring and access to top-tier talent, while flexible terms help you save time and reduce costs.




Connect with us and learn more:
HighlightTA on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/highlightta

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



Please note: Zipline is 100% remote and accepts applicants from Canada, US & internationally. This role would require you to work in a European Time Zone.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Manager at Zipline, you'll fill a critical role managing ongoing customer relationships and maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. You'll be the primary point of contact for assigned accounts, responsible for onboarding, adoption, renewals, and expansion. You'll also act as the voice of the customer internally, partnering with Implementation, Support, and Product to make sure customers get lasting value from Zipline. This role is built for someone who takes initiative, thinks independently, and gets genuine energy from building relationships and making customers win.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Support/Lead the onboarding of new customers and ensure a successful Zipline launch to their users.

 - Act as the primary point of contact for assigned customers and serve as the voice of the customer within Zipline.

 - Build strong relationships across each customer's organization, including executive sponsors and operational stakeholders, by understanding their goals, culture, and decision-making structure.

 - Advise customers on best practices and continuous improvement opportunities to drive long-term success with Zipline.

 - Educate customers on new features, enhancements, bug fixes, and relevant Zipline updates.

 - Partner with Implementation, Support, Product, and technical stakeholders to ensure smooth onboarding, handoff, and post-launch optimization.

 - Maintain a structured success plan following implementation, including adoption milestones, business goals, and growth opportunities.

 - Own customer health, renewal readiness, churn-risk identification, and mitigation across assigned accounts.

 - Use customer goals, usage data, reporting, and performance metrics to drive adoption, quantify ROI, and inform account strategy.

 - Identify expansion opportunities and help customers build business cases and budgets to support renewals and upsell opportunities.

 - Help customers evaluate usage, ROI, and other key metrics that can be used internally and externally.

 - Lead challenging customer situations with professionalism, urgency, and sound judgment.

 - Work with Marketing to develop case studies, customer video clips, quotes, and other sales and marketing assets that showcase customer success stories.





WHAT WE LOOK FOR

 - 4+ years of experience managing customer accounts in a B2B SaaS environment, including ownership of renewals and expansion opportunities.

 - Retail experience preferred, particularly in store operations or headquarters environments.

 - Exceptional communication, presentation, listening, and writing skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels and translate complex concepts into clear, intuitive guidance.

 - Bilingual: Italian/English preferred; Spanish, French, or German a plus.

 - Strong relationship-building and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work effectively across customers and cross-functional internal teams.

 - Strong analytical skills, with experience using customer data and reporting to assess account health and inform strategy.

 - Strong project-management and organizational skills, with the ability to coordinate across technical and business stakeholders.

 - Experience supporting customer onboarding

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



Please note: Zipline is 100% remote and accepts applicants from Canada, US & internationally. This role would require you to work in a European Time Zone.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Manager at Zipline, you'll fill a critical role managing ongoing customer relationships and maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. You'll be the primary point of contact for assigned accounts, responsible for onboarding, adoption, renewals, and expansion. You'll also act as the voice of the customer internally, partnering with Implementation, Support, and Product to make sure customers get lasting value from Zipline. This role is built for someone who takes initiative, thinks independently, and gets genuine energy from building relationships and making customers win.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Support/Lead the onboarding of new customers and ensure a successful Zipline launch to their users.

 - Act as the primary point of contact for assigned customers and serve as the voice of the customer within Zipline.

 - Build strong relationships across each customer's organization, including executive sponsors and operational stakeholders, by understanding their goals, culture, and decision-making structure.

 - Advise customers on best practices and continuous improvement opportunities to drive long-term success with Zipline.

 - Educate customers on new features, enhancements, bug fixes, and relevant Zipline updates.

 - Partner with Implementation, Support, Product, and technical stakeholders to ensure smooth onboarding, handoff, and post-launch optimization.

 - Maintain a structured success plan following implementation, including adoption milestones, business goals, and growth opportunities.

 - Own customer health, renewal readiness, churn-risk identification, and mitigation across assigned accounts.

 - Use customer goals, usage data, reporting, and performance metrics to drive adoption, quantify ROI, and inform account strategy.

 - Identify expansion opportunities and help customers build business cases and budgets to support renewals and upsell opportunities.

 - Help customers evaluate usage, ROI, and other key metrics that can be used internally and externally.

 - Lead challenging customer situations with professionalism, urgency, and sound judgment.

 - Work with Marketing to develop case studies, customer video clips, quotes, and other sales and marketing assets that showcase customer success stories.





WHAT WE LOOK FOR

 - 4+ years of experience managing customer accounts in a B2B SaaS environment, including ownership of renewals and expansion opportunities.

 - Retail experience preferred, particularly in store operations or headquarters environments.

 - Exceptional communication, presentation, listening, and writing skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels and translate complex concepts into clear, intuitive guidance.

 - Bilingual: Italian/English preferred; Spanish, French, or German a plus.

 - Strong relationship-building and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work effectively across customers and cross-functional internal teams.

 - Strong analytical skills, with experience using customer data and reporting to assess account health and inform strategy.

 - Strong project-management and organizational skills, with the ability to coordinate across technical and business stakeholders.

 - Experience supporting customer onboarding

source_jobs.title: Intermediate Front End Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Zipline is looking for a Front-End Engineer who lives at the intersection of design and code. You won't just implement specs, you'll shape the UI itself, building directly in code with the same care a designer brings to a canvas. You'll work closely with our product, engineering, and design teams to deliver polished, performant interfaces across our platform. Your primary focus will be front-end development using Ruby on Rails, Hotwire (Turbo & Stimulus), and modern JavaScript, with a strong secondary focus on user experience and design craft.





WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Collaborate with engineers, designers, and product managers to build functional, beautiful user interfaces. Often working design-forward in code rather than waiting for pixel-perfect handoffs especially in the world of AI

 - Develop, enhance, and maintain responsive web applications using Ruby on Rails, Turbo, Stimulus, and JavaScript.

 - Contribute to and evolve Zipline's design system with View Components, treating it as living code.

 - Build and iterate on prototypes directly in code, using the design system as your foundation.

 - Use AI-assisted development tools (e.g. Claude Code, Copilot) as a natural part of your workflow to move faster without sacrificing quality.

 - Write clean, maintainable, and efficient code that scales to keep large teams productive.

 - Work with other developers to improve usability, accessibility, and performance.

 - Diagnose and resolve front-end issues to provide a smooth user experience.

 - Optimize applications for maximum speed and design mobile-first features, including Turbo Native mobile experiences.

 - Write clear functional requirement documents and guides that other engineers can pick up and run with.

 - Stay current on front-end tooling, patterns, and AI-augmented development practices.




WHAT WE LOOK FOR


MUST HAVES

 - 3+ years experience in a front-end developer role.

 - Professional experience with Ruby on Rails, JavaScript, CSS, and HTML at scale.

 - Hands-on experience with Turbo, Stimulus, or the broader Hotwire ecosystem.

 - You can look at a UI, see what's off, and fix it in code without needing a designer to redline it for you.

 - Experience building within and contributing to a design system with View Components and Lookbook

 - Comfort using AI coding tools as part of your daily development workflow.

 - A problem-solving mindset that finds elegant solutions when working within the constraints of a design system.

 - Refined time management and proactive communication skills that let you do your best work in an asynchronous, fully remote environment.




NICE TO HAVES

 - Experience with Turbo Native (iOS/Android).

 - Experience with React or Vue.js component architectures.

 - A passion for teaching and mentoring other engineers on front-end craft.

 - Familiarity with prompt engineering or building AI-augmented features into product UIs.

 - Retail industry experience.




MORE ABOUT YOU

 - You take pride in your craft. Clean code, polished UI, and attention to detail aren't afterthoughts, they're the baseline.

 - You think of design as something you do in code, not something someone else hands you.

 - You're curious about AI tooling and excited about what it means for how we build software, not threatened by it.

 - Outstanding listening and writing skills, with a knack for translating complex concepts into simple,

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT HIGHLIGHTTA

HighlightTA is dedicated to delivering fast, flexible, and cost-effective talent solutions that help companies build exceptional teams. Our team of skilled professionals works closely with clients to understand their unique hiring needs and provide tailored solutions. As former in-house talent leaders, we saw that many businesses struggle with hiring demands, layoffs, and inconsistent growth. So we started HighlightTA with a simple goal: to help companies grow sustainably and access top talent experts easily.

Join us to be part of a dynamic and innovative company that values collaboration, growth, and creativity.




WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR

We are seeking an experienced Senior Technical Recruiter with a strong background in scaling companies and building engineering and product teams.

You are highly motivated, productive, and data-driven, thriving in fast-paced environments where multiple high-priority roles need to be filled quickly. While your core focus is technical hiring, you bring versatility and adaptability to take on non-technical roles as business needs evolve.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of full-cycle recruitment experience for a SaaS based business

 - 2+ years specializing in technical roles (e.g., Software Engineers, Data Scientists, DevOps, Product Managers)

 - Flexibility to recruit across non-technical functions (e.g., operations, sales, leadership)

 - You are comfortable using AI tools as part of your regular workflow. You can name the tools you rely on, describe real use cases, and show genuine enthusiasm for what they make possible.

 - Data-driven mindset, using metrics and insights to refine recruitment strategies

 - Highly motivated and able to handle multiple high-priority searches in fast-moving environments

 - Strong partnerships with Engineering, Product, and Technical Leadership

 - Dedication to diversity, inclusion, and delivering a seamless candidate experience




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with scaling companies to expand their technical and product teams

 - Build strong hiring manager partnerships, acting as an embedded talent partner who understands team goals and drives searches forward together

 - Provide candidates with an excellent, transparent, and engaging hiring experience

 - Support startups with tailored and flexible recruiting approaches

 - Manage recruitment across technical and non-technical roles as needed

 - Operate within Slack-based workflows across internal teams and client workspaces

 - Source passive candidates through advanced techniques and technical networks

 - Facilitate technical assessments and structured interview processes

 - Deliver insights on hiring trends, compensation, and market dynamics

 - Balance multiple urgent searches simultaneously with high productivity




WHAT WE OFFER

 - Career progression: Opportunities for advancement within HighlightTA

 - Guidance & training: Programs to support your professional growth

 - Flexible work: Arrangements that fit your lifestyle

 - Competitive compensation: Attractive packages that reward performance

 - 100% remote WFH: Work from anywhere with a fully remote setup

source_jobs.title: Director of Product, Core Business
source_jobs.description:
About Zipline

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



The Role

Zipline’s core product is the engine behind how the world’s best retailers operate their frontline, and it’s evolving fast. As we build toward an AI-first frontline operations platform, the Director of Product, Core products is the leader accountable for making that evolution real across everything we ship today.

This role collaborates on the product strategy, team performance, and business outcomes for Zipline’s core platform: the tools used daily by 620,000+ frontline workers across 200+ world-class brands. You will lead a formed team of product managers, own the 12–18 month roadmap, and serve as the connective tissue between day-to-day execution and Zipline’s five active strategic pillars.

This is a senior people leadership role for someone who has been here before. You will inherit a talented, formed team and be expected to run it: elevating craft, moving decision-making down, and creating the conditions for PMs to operate with autonomy and accountability, driving a roadmap that is creating revenue for the business, while bringing the team forward with AI-enabled workflows.

You report to the SVP, Product and act as a peer to the Director of Product, Strategic Ventures. Together you represent the full span of Zipline’s product ambition: you execute on what exists and evolves today; your peer bets on what comes next. You are the strategic execution layer the SVP needs in order to operate at a company-level altitude.



What You’ll Do



Own the core product roadmap

Define and maintain the 12–18 month product roadmap across Zipline’s core platform pillars. Translate Zipline’s vision and strategy into clear, sequenced, evidence-backed product investments. Ensure every PM on your team owns a strategy (not just a backlog) and can speak to outcomes, not features. Build rigorous roadmap narratives that give executives and stakeholders clarity on where we are going and why. Be capable of representing Zipline’s core product story compellingly on stage at Summer Camp, and other various customer and prospect-focused functions, as well as in customer conversations, and in cross-functional planning.



Lead and develop a high-performing PM team

Manage, coach, and develop a team of product managers across Zipline’s core product pillars. Apply strong situational leadership, meeting each PM where they are, stretching those who are ready, supporting those who are developing, and holding the bar across the team. Drive the IC-to-leader transition on your team: build accountability, strategic thinking, and autonomous decision-making as norms. Hold a high bar on product craft: the quality of discovery, the rigor of prioritization, the clarity of outcome definition. Over time, identify and develop the next layer of leadership within your team.



Drive strategy-to-execution alignment across Engineering, Product, and Design (EPD)

Own the translation of company strategy into product decisions, PM priorities, and quarterly plans. Serve as the escalation path for your PMs: unblock decisions, resolve cross-functional friction, and keep teams moving. Partner with Engineering and Design leadership to run a healthy, high-functioning EPD triad for the core business. Move decision-making down: structure your team so that day-to-day execution runs without requiring SVP involvement, and surface only what genuinely requires it. Flag risks, resource gaps, and strategic misalignment proactiv

source_jobs.title: Content Strategist + Producer
source_jobs.description:
About the Role

We are looking for a creative, strategic, and digitally driven content marketing professional. This is more than a marketing role, it's an opportunity to help shape the voice, digital presence, and growth strategy of our organisation. If you thrive in a fast-paced environment, love combining creativity with analytics, and want to make a visible impact across digital platforms, social media, branding, recruiting, and customer engagement, this role offers the perfect mix of strategy and hands-on execution.

You'll work directly with leadership, collaborate across departments, leverage AI marketing technologies, and play a key role in elevating the brand nationwide. As a key member of the marketing team, you'll report to our Creative Director to execute high-impact digital and content initiatives.



Key Responsibilities:

Content Production & Ownership

 - Serve as the primary content producer for the brand, owning written, video, and creative content from concept through delivery, including scripting, production, editing, and publishing

 - Support production of written content across digital articles, email campaigns, social copy, and print collateral, always clear, on-brand, and strategically relevant

Content Strategy & Audience Alignment

 - Develop and execute content strategies that speak to target personas, industries, and communities, while staying tightly aligned to Brand Marketing priorities

 - Create compelling content tailored for senior b2b buyers, and manage paid content with third party influencers

Social & Channel Growth

 - Develop and lead our owned podcast as well as third party placements

 - Manage and grow the company's social media presence across LinkedIn, YouTube, and whatever comes next, adapting format, tone, and cadence to each platform

Collaboration, Analytics & AI

 - Collaborate with the broader Marketing team to integrate content across events, paid, organic, and owned channels

 - Bring a test-and-learn mindset, regularly analysing content performance, extracting insights, and letting the data evolve your approach

 - Use AI-powered marketing tools and emerging technologies to streamline content creation, reporting, and campaign performance



Who You Are

 - Creative, strategic, and digitally driven, with a genuine love for combining creativity and analytics

 - Energised by a fast-paced environment and motivated by making a visible, brand-wide impact

 - Independent and self-directed: you manage your own production schedule, deadlines, and content calendar

 - A confident communicator who presents concepts, explains creative decisions, and takes feedback constructively

 - Data-informed: you let performance insights guide what you make next



Requirements

Must Haves

 - 4 to 6 years of experience in content creation, social media, or a related marketing role, with a portfolio that shows multi-channel work

 - Solid writing across formats: social captions, email copy, digital articles, and print, with a consistent, compelling brand voice

 - Channel fluency: you know what performs on LinkedIn vs YouTube vs TikTok, email vs blogs, and video vs reports, and adapt format, tone, and cadence accordingly

 - Creative tools fluency with Claude and Figma, or equivalent, able to concept and execute without leaning on external resources for every asset

 - Data-comfortable: you read platform analytics, A/B test thumbnails, copy, and CTAs, and translate engagement and reach metrics into better content decisions

Nice to Haves

 - Hands-on experience with AI tools for content ideation, production efficiency, or personalisation workflows




ABOUT HIGHLIGHTTA

This confidential opportunity is presented by HighlightTA, your on-demand talent team. We make hiring easy with flexible, on-demand talent solutions. We partner with startups and scale-ups to embed talent partners, lead recruitment projects, and manage full talent functions. Our data-driven approach ensures fast hiring and access

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer, Design Systems & Foundations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

Zipline is the operating system for the frontline, used by hundreds of thousands of store associates and managers every day at some of the world's leading retail brands. As our product and design teams continue to grow, we're investing in the systems and foundations that help us build consistent, high-quality experiences at scale.

We're looking for a Staff Product Designer to lead our Design Systems & Foundations work. This is a senior individual contributor role focused on more than maintaining a component library. You'll build and evolve the design systems, product frameworks, and foundational infrastructure that support product development across Zipline.

Your work will help designers, engineers, and PMs move faster, make better decisions, and create more consistent experiences across the product. You'll partner closely with design leadership, engineering, and product teams to shape how Zipline builds and scales.

 


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own and evolve ZUI, Zipline's design system, including component architecture, design tokens, accessibility standards, documentation, and implementation quality. Partner closely with engineering to ensure the system evolves alongside our product and technical architecture.

 - Establish shared interaction patterns, mental models, and design principles that create consistency across the product. Translate learnings from product work into reusable systems, frameworks, and foundations that can scale across teams.

 - Build the documentation, guidance, and decision-making frameworks that help designers, engineers, and PMs work independently and consistently. Drive adoption of the design system and product foundations across the organization.

 - Contribute to complex product initiatives, influence long-term product direction, and help define how emerging technologies, including AI, are incorporated into our design practice and workflows.

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience leading or significantly contributing to a design system at scale

 - Strong systems thinking across interaction design, workflows, information architecture, and visual design

 - Proven ability to drive alignment and adoption across cross-functional teams

 - Excellent visual and interaction design craft

 - Strong partnership with engineering and understanding of implementation constraints

 - Comfort working in ambiguity and shaping long-term product foundations

 - Interest in exploring how AI can improve design workflows and product development

 


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience designing for frontline, retail, or operational workflows

 - Experience in a platform, systems, or other cross-cutting design role

 - Familiarity with modern design system governance and documentation practices

 - Experience creating structured design context that can be leveraged by AI tools

 


WHY THIS ROLE MATTERS

This role sits at the center of how Zipline designs and builds products. You'll help define the systems, standards, and foundations that influence work across the entire organization, creating leverage far beyond any single feature or product area.

 


COMPENSATION

At Zipline, we're committed to paying our team fairly, transparently, and in a way that supports growth. This role is targeting an annual salary within the US of $178,075 USD to $217,625 USD.

The broad salary ranges reflect the growth someone will experience in the role—from developing s

source_jobs.title: Senior UX/UI Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT STICKERYOU

StickerYou is a leading global e-commerce company for custom printed products such as stickers, labels, decals, temporary tattoos and more. Our proprietary platform infuses automation technology and creativity to empower anyone to make the best custom products in any size, shape and quantity. Businesses and consumers can elevate their marketing, packaging, décor or boost their personal expression with our professional-grade products. We believe in the enormous power of customization. We are a smart, creative, helpful and passionate group dedicated to growing the most talented and engaging people in Toronto, Canada. We are a global company utilizing software and advanced digital manufacturing. We proudly make custom products locally. We make what matters stick!




THE ROLE

As a key member of StickerYou’s growing Development Department, we’re looking for a Senior UI/UX Designer. This person will report directly to the VP of Technology & Digital Products. This position will heavily collaborate with our VP of Marketing, and work collaboratively with other teams. This person will drive the user experience strategy for the StickerYou.com http://StickerYou.com e-commerce site by working both independently and collaboratively with others. This person will be challenged to use their critical thinking and problem-solving skills to come up with creative solutions that improve the user experience across all channels.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Craft the overall user experience strategy, design system, and content architecture for the site

 - Oversee a complete overhaul of site journeys and navigation

 - Create end-to-end designs including low-fidelity user interface sketches/wireframes through to high-fidelity pixel perfect visual design mockups and prototypes

 - Monitor analytics on user behaviour and site performance, continuously seeking opportunities to deliver a superior and higher converting experience

 - Develop a proficient understanding of StickerYou's products, customers and competition by leading and participating in user research, competitive analysis and other design thinking activities

 - Apply user-centered design best practices and methodologies as well as mobile-first/responsive design thinking

 - Work within tight deadlines without compromising quality

 - Individual contributor role




WHAT YOU BRING TO THE TEAM

 - 5+ years in UX Design, Digital Product Design or Visual/UI design

 - A strong portfolio of projects which demonstrate creative, user-centered design solutions to complex problems, especially in an e-commerce context

 - Bachelor's and/or Master's degree in Design, User Experience or a related field

 - High attention to detail and strong organizational skills

 - Ability to take responsibility for design of solutions from concept to completion

 - Ability to communicate clearly and effectively across departments

 - Proficient and effective use of industry-standard UX and UI design tools, including Figma, Sketch, Adobe Creative Suite and XD and UXPin

 - Understanding of HTML/CSS/JS capabilities and limitations

 - Knowledge of printing industry considered an asset

 - Bachelor's or Master's degree in Design, HCI, Psychology, or a related field is an asset




KEY ATTRIBUTES WE LOOK FOR

High Standards: You are organized and self-motivated. You ensure your work adheres to corporate and industry standards.

Problem Solving: You have the ability to view problems from a holistic perspective and focus on finding sustainable and efficient solutions, thinking through possible implications and results from both the business and customer perspective. Balances the strategic and tactical.

Aptitude for Learning: You are a quick learner who looks for opportunities to find new and better ways to do things. You are eager to expand your knowledge of accounting and our operations.

Team Player: You communicate openly, timely and in a clear manner any issue with your manager, co-workers and anyone el

source_jobs.title: Senior Ruby on Rails Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Zipline

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees—from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



About the Role

We're looking for a Senior Ruby on Rails Engineer to join our team as the surface area of our product and infrastructure continues to scale with rapid customer growth. This is a great opportunity for someone excited about shaping robust systems—and contributing to AI-powered initiatives such as automatic content creation.



What You’ll Do

 - Defining and improving useful APIs for our customers

 - Design, develop, and maintain robust and scalable features for our web-based applications

 - Maintain and enhance existing features, adapting them for an evolving market

 - Evangelize coding, testing and performance best practices with the rest of the team

 - Collaborate with our product, design and customer success teams on new features that customers will love
   
   

What We're Looking For

 - You have significant relevant production experience with large-scale Ruby on Rails applications

 - You have great written and verbal communication skills

 - You prefer taking projects from inception to completion and are outcome-oriented

 - You have a strong understanding of systems architecture, database design, and the Ruby language

 - You are proactive with communication and have no problem managing your time as a remote employee

 - You are proud of your craft, and enjoy and value clean code that scales to keep large teams productive

 - You have an interest in—or experience with—AI/ML technologies, especially in content generation or data structuring



Technologies You'll Work With

 - Ruby on Rails

 - Postgres, Redis, Elasticsearch

 - TypeScript, JavaScript, React, Webpack

 - AWS, Heroku, Ansible, Digital Ocean

 - GitHub, Slack, Google Drive, JIRA

 - Claude Code
   
   

Compensation

At Zipline, we're committed to paying our team fairly, transparently, and in a way that supports growth. This role is targeting an annual salary within the United Kingdom of £79,424 to £97,074.



The broad salary ranges reflect the growth someone will experience in the role—from developing skills to reaching expertise within their career level. We've found that hiring team members with the expectation of being accomplished in the role sets them up for success, which for this position on the United Kingdom band is £79,424. Salaries below this point typically reflect someone with potential but still building the necessary skills, while salaries above are for individuals already excelling in the role, with a deep understanding of Zipline's impact on customers, product, and company.



At Zipline, you'll continue to have career development conversations with your manager to ensure your compensation grows as you advance.



Our salary ranges are determined by market, factoring in our revenue and employee size and paying competitively. In higher cost of living markets, we apply 5-10% multipliers on our ranges, depending on the location, to maintain competitive compensation and reflective of the increased expenses our team members face in those areas. While we primarily hire in Canada and the USA, we are a global employer, and all hiring regions will use the same methodology to determine fair and transparent pay.



Transparency is key to building trust in our compensation process. To better understand how this range is determined, we encourage you to ask as many questions as you'd like about compensation during the interview process to ensure clarity and confidence.

source_jobs.title: Account Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ZIPLINE

Zipline is a well-funded, rapidly growing SaaS company transforming how frontline teams work. We empower the world’s leading brands across retail, healthcare, logistics, and beyond to connect, align, and inspire their employees- from headquarters to the front lines. Our customers consistently give us best-in-class NPS and CSAT scores, and they rave about how Zipline makes their teams’ lives easier and their businesses more effective. We’re also a fully remote company, with passionate employees across the U.S., Canada, and around the globe.



Please note: Zipline is 100% remote and accepts applicants from Canada, US & internationally. This role would require you to work in a European Time Zone.




ABOUT THE ROLE

As an Account Manager at Zipline, you'll fill a critical role managing ongoing customer relationships and maintaining high levels of customer satisfaction. You'll be the primary point of contact for assigned accounts, responsible for onboarding, adoption, renewals, and expansion. You'll also act as the voice of the customer internally, partnering with Implementation, Support, and Product to make sure customers get lasting value from Zipline. This role is built for someone who takes initiative, thinks independently, and gets genuine energy from building relationships and making customers win.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Support/Lead the onboarding of new customers and ensure a successful Zipline launch to their users.

 - Act as the primary point of contact for assigned customers and serve as the voice of the customer within Zipline.

 - Build strong relationships across each customer's organization, including executive sponsors and operational stakeholders, by understanding their goals, culture, and decision-making structure.

 - Advise customers on best practices and continuous improvement opportunities to drive long-term success with Zipline.

 - Educate customers on new features, enhancements, bug fixes, and relevant Zipline updates.

 - Partner with Implementation, Support, Product, and technical stakeholders to ensure smooth onboarding, handoff, and post-launch optimization.

 - Maintain a structured success plan following implementation, including adoption milestones, business goals, and growth opportunities.

 - Own customer health, renewal readiness, churn-risk identification, and mitigation across assigned accounts.

 - Use customer goals, usage data, reporting, and performance metrics to drive adoption, quantify ROI, and inform account strategy.

 - Identify expansion opportunities and help customers build business cases and budgets to support renewals and upsell opportunities.

 - Help customers evaluate usage, ROI, and other key metrics that can be used internally and externally.

 - Lead challenging customer situations with professionalism, urgency, and sound judgment.

 - Work with Marketing to develop case studies, customer video clips, quotes, and other sales and marketing assets that showcase customer success stories.





WHAT WE LOOK FOR

 - 4+ years of experience managing customer accounts in a B2B SaaS environment, including ownership of renewals and expansion opportunities.

 - Retail experience preferred, particularly in store operations or headquarters environments.

 - Exceptional communication, presentation, listening, and writing skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels and translate complex concepts into clear, intuitive guidance.

 - Bilingual: Italian/English preferred; Spanish, French, or German a plus.

 - Strong relationship-building and collaboration skills, with a proven ability to work effectively across customers and cross-functional internal teams.

 - Strong analytical skills, with experience using customer data and reporting to assess account health and inform strategy.

 - Strong project-management and organizational skills, with the ability to coordinate across technical and business stakeholders.

 - Experience supporting customer onboarding

source_jobs.title: Senior Site Reliability Engineer
source_jobs.description:
COMPANY OVERVIEW

Our client is a fast-growing SaaS company building tools used by organizations globally to support key business operations. The company has made AI a core part of its product strategy, using it to cut down on manual work and tailor the experience to each user, and operates with a culture built on ownership, autonomy, and collaboration.



ROLE OVERVIEW

This role sits within a globally distributed SRE function responsible for keeping a large scale, multi-tenant SaaS platform running reliably for customers around the world. You'll take a hands-on lead role in high severity incident response while also shaping the underlying infrastructure that supports the platform's scale. The position blends deep technical ownership with cross-team influence, giving you room to fix root causes, not just symptoms, and to raise the reliability bar across the wider engineering org.



WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

 - Take point on major incidents, coordinating the response across engineering teams and making the real-time calls needed to restore service quickly.

 - Hold ownership over key pieces of the shared infrastructure, working continuously to improve its resilience and ability to scale.

 - Run structured reliability work such as capacity forecasting, performance tuning, and controlled failure testing.

 - Improve how the org detects problems before customers do, by refining metrics, alerting, and overall observability.

 - Run thorough retrospectives after incidents and make sure the resulting action items actually get built, not just logged.

 - Support less experienced SREs through code and design review, pairing, and ongoing feedback, and help spread strong reliability practices across Product and Engineering more broadly.



WHAT YOU BRING

 - 4 to 8 years working in SRE, DevOps, or production engineering roles within SaaS environments.

 - Solid, practical grounding in Linux, containerized systems, and cloud native infrastructure, including hands-on experience with a major cloud provider and its networking layer.

 - Comfort writing code or scripts to manage infrastructure, with a genuine preference for infrastructure as code over manual configuration.

 - Real experience building and running CI/CD pipelines, observability tooling, and version control workflows at scale.

 - A track record of staying level headed during live incidents, and the ability to explain technical issues clearly to people who aren't engineers.



ABOUT HIGHLIGHTTA

This confidential opportunity is presented by HighlightTA, your on-demand talent team. We make hiring easy with flexible, on-demand talent solutions. We partner with startups and scale-ups to embed talent partners, lead recruitment projects, and manage full talent functions. Our data-driven approach ensures fast hiring and access to top-tier talent, while flexible terms help you save time and reduce costs.


Connect with us and learn more:
HighlightTA on LinkedIn https://www.linkedin.com/company/highlightta

notes: Multiple companies listed, including Zipline, Neon One, and HighlightTA. Zipline appears to be a well-funded SaaS company with a strong focus on remote work.
